# UC-22.12.34 · IT risk register residual score movement for financial reporting risks

## Description

Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "IT risk register residual score movement for financial reporting risks" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX risk attestation.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOX ITGC compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes summary and sourcetypes risk:register. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=summary`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=summary sourcetype=risk:register domain="IT" earliest=-365d
| eval uc_tag="22.12.34"
| eval delta=residual_score-prev_residual_score
| where abs(delta)>=1
| table risk_id, risk_title, residual_score, delta
```

Understanding this SPL

**IT risk register residual score movement for financial reporting risks (SOX risk)** — Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "IT risk register residual score movement for financial reporting risks" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX risk attestation.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: summary; **sourcetype**: risk:register. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=summary, sourcetype=risk:register, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **delta** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where abs(delta)>=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Output fields: delta, residual_score, risk_id, risk_title, uc_tag.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ERP audit trail (SAP, Oracle ERP), reconcile to ITSM or change records where needed, and confirm results, field extractions, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table, Single value, Bar chart

## SPL

```spl
index=summary sourcetype=risk:register domain="IT" earliest=-365d
| eval uc_tag="22.12.34"
| eval delta=residual_score-prev_residual_score
| where abs(delta)>=1
| table risk_id, risk_title, residual_score, delta
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Time chart, Table, Single value, Bar chart. Key fields: `delta`, `residual_score`, `risk_id`, `risk_title`, `uc_tag`.
